summary.en: Experience the dramatic natural beauty of the Oregon coast where it meets a quaint, arts-focused village. The air is crisp with salt spray, and the colossal Haystack Rock serves as the undeniable focal point.

description.en: This 235-foot sea stack is a protected National Wildlife Refuge. At low tide, you can walk to its base to explore vibrant tide pools filled with sea stars and anemones.
A quieter, local's access point to the beach, offering an expansive perspective of Haystack Rock and the coastline with fewer crowds.
A hidden-in-plain-sight gem offering a quiet, wooded refuge with trails winding through a coastal forest of Sitka spruce and western hemlock.
Located where Ecola Creek meets the Pacific, this park offers great birdwatching and dramatic views of the Tillamook Rock Lighthouse and northern headlands.

prompt.en: Find a stretch of wet sand at low tide and capture a perfect, mirror-like reflection of the sky, a sea stack, or the coastal cliffs.
Photograph the most interesting piece of driftwood you can find. Focus on its unique shape, texture, and how it has been weathered by the ocean.
Take a photo that emphasizes the immense scale of the Pacific coastline. Include a person or small object in the frame to show how vast the beach and sea stacks are.
Capture a detail of the town's cedar-shingled architecture. Look for interesting angles, textures, or how the wood contrasts with the coastal sky.
Find a tide pool and take a close-up photo of the ecosystem within. Try to capture the color and life, like anemones, shells, or small crabs.
Find a spot where the coastal forest comes close to the beach. Take a photo that captures the contrast between the lush green trees and the sandy shore.
Take a short video capturing the constant, powerful sound of the surf. Pan slowly across the horizon to show the waves rolling in.
Take a selfie on a contemplative walk along the beach. Your expression should capture the peaceful, dramatic mood of the Oregon coast.
During the golden hour (sunrise or sunset), capture a photo of the long shadows cast by the sea stacks or people on the beach.
From the town's streets, find a view of the ocean or Haystack Rock framed by buildings, trees, or an alleyway.
Photograph one of the many sea birds in the area. Try to capture it in flight, perched on a rock, or searching for food along the shoreline.
Take an artistic photo of footprints in the sand leading towards the ocean or a landmark, telling a story of a walk just taken.
